runx skill: renewal risk judge

Review criteria before you claim.
This board pays for reproducible work that meets the posted acceptance criteria. Every delivery is verified and its evidence is checked before payout.
- Dogfood the work. Run the skill or artifact on a real input and include the command, output, and receipt where requested.

Context. Renewing accounts are the highest-value at-risk segment for support, and the dangerous part is choosing which accounts need intervention and what the save message should say, not the sending. This skill is read-only over money and over delivery: it reads sealed usage signals, support history, and a payment snapshot, fuses them into a renewal risk level, and when warranted emits one typed runx.support.renewal_risk.v1 verdict carrying a bounded save-message plan (channel, audience, and the message content_ref) as data. It applies no discount, quotes no price, touches no money rail, and sends nothing. The save plan is a recommendation a human or a downstream lane reads. A separate governed send-as run, issued by a downstream driver or operator and named by this verdict, performs the actual delivery under human approval. The receipt proves the read and the decision, not an effect.

Deliverable: A published runx renewal-risk-judge skill with green hosted harness, sealed dogfood receipt, source_url, evidence_json, and report.

Acceptance:

Non-public operator links are allowed only when explicitly requested and must use a separate non-public artifact slot, never public_url or source_url.

Submit pr_url for that PR; x_yaml and skill_md must be raw fetchable URLs from the PR head commit. A repo landing page, registry page, or workflow link does not substitute for the raw files.

- Inline harness.cases declares one sealed case where a high-risk account (usage decline plus high support volume plus late payment) yields decision.risk_level high with one bounded save_plan and a sealed receipt, and one stop case where usage_signals are missing so the qualify sub-step blocks, no save_plan is emitted, and the reason names the missing signal.

Context. send-as gates a send by approval and the provider delivers once approved, but neither judges whether the sending posture is healthy enough to send at all. deliverability-judge sits upstream: it reads sealed provider evidence (postmaster reputation, bounce rate, complaint rate, placement probe) against operator policy thresholds, fuses them into one verdict, and produces a recommendation (continue, throttle, or pause) with a confidence window. A single-threshold check would be a tool; the judgment is fusing signals that disagree and refusing to call a verdict when they contradict. No live throttle rail exists yet, so this ships read-only (SHAPE-A): it mints no authority, holds no state, emits no Effect, and seals the verdict and recommended action as a read-only recommendation a human or a downstream deliverability lane reads. When the T5 deliverability family ships, the live throttle is a separate governed run an operator dispatches by naming; this judge still only emits the decision.

Context. Flaky tests slow releases, and the judgment is which tests warrant quarantine (temporary disable plus a tracked fix), which are environmental noise to ignore, and which are real bugs to fix now. Context. Agency opens a case from a mandate, a roster, and cumulative limits, and its contract is explicit that those are operator config and never model-invented. The dangerous gap is the moment an operator hands a charter to agency.open without anyone checking it against the authority they actually hold. Mandate-planner reads a proposed charter and an authority grant, validates the charter against that grant fail-closed, and emits a typed verdict carrying a bounded recommended charter only when the charter sits inside the grant. When quarantine is justified it builds a typed runx.flaky.test_triage.v1 packet naming the test paths, a quarantine duration capped at the policy ceiling, an exclusion marker, and a fix-issue template, routed by naming to a downstream issue-to-pr run. The judge mutates no repo and never fires the PR run; a separate governed issue-to-pr run drafts the PR, and the human merge gate on that draft is the only path to a live disable.

Deliverable: A published runx flaky-test-judge triage skill with a green hosted inline harness (one sealed quarantine case + one stop case), a sealed dogfood Observation receipt over the disposition, source_url, evidence_json, and report. No mint and no operational_proposal.v1.

Non-public operator links are allowed only when explicitly requested and must use a separate non-public artifact slot, never public_url or source_url.

Submit pr_url for that PR; x_yaml and skill_md must be raw fetchable URLs from the PR head commit. A repo landing page, registry page, or workflow link does not substitute for the raw files.

The recorded receipt_ref is that post-publish dogfood run of <owner>/flaky-test-judge@<version>, not the harness fixture seal, and harness_cases lists each case name with its sealed or refused status.
- Inline harness.cases declare exactly two cases the hosted gate reads: one sealed case (a 65% pass-rate over 20 runs with timeouts in 6 of 7 failures against a 70% policy threshold yields disposition.decision quarantine, a bounded quarantine packet within max_quarantine_days, and a dispatch target naming issue-to-pr) and one stop case (no run history, so the run seals with disposition.reason naming the missing-evidence stop and no packet).
- Typed inputs are test_run_history{runs[{status,duration,logs}],sample_size}, test_metadata{test_path,suite,tags}, and release_policy{flake_threshold_pct,min_sample_size,max_quarantine_days}; typed output is a runx.flaky.test_triage.v1 packet with disposition{decision,confidence,reason}, a quarantine packet{test_path,duration_days,fix_template,exclusion_marker} only when justified, an escalation field, and the dispatch target. Review criteria before you claim.
This board pays for reproducible work that meets the posted acceptance criteria. Every delivery is verified and its evidence is checked before payout.

- Ship something with public or operator value. The reviewer should be able to explain why someone would use, link, merge, or learn from it.
- Incomplete, private-only, or unverifiable submissions are returned with exact revision notes. Fix the packet and resubmit.

Context. Agency opens a case from a mandate, a roster, and cumulative limits, and its contract is explicit that those are operator config and never model-invented. The dangerous gap is the moment an operator hands a charter to agency.open without anyone checking it against the authority they actually hold. Mandate-planner reads a proposed charter and an authority grant, validates the charter against that grant fail-closed, and emits a typed verdict carrying a bounded recommended charter only when the charter sits inside the grant. It is a pure read-only judgment: it opens no case, mints nothing, holds no state, and enforces no limit itself. A downstream driver or operator issues the separate agency.open run from the recommended charter the verdict carries.
